articles.headline: Google DeepMind Unveils Gemini Ultra 2 with Real-Time Reasoning
articles.summary: Google's latest flagship model demonstrates near-human performance on complex multi-step reasoning benchmarks, with new capabilities in live video understanding and agentic task execution.

articles.headline: AMD Launches Ryzen 9000 Series with 3D V-Cache for AI Workloads
articles.summary: AMD's newest desktop processors feature a redesigned cache architecture optimised for local AI inference, delivering 2× throughput improvement over the previous generation at the same TDP.

articles.headline: Microsoft Copilot Now Embedded Across All Windows 11 System Apps
articles.summary: The latest Windows 11 update brings Copilot deep into File Explorer, Settings, and Taskbar — allowing users to ask questions, rename files in bulk, and summarise notifications using natural language.

articles.headline: S&P 500 Edges Toward Record High as Inflation Data Cools
articles.summary: Markets rallied broadly after April's CPI reading came in at 2.8%, below analyst expectations, sparking optimism around a potential Fed rate cut in September 2026.

articles.headline: NVIDIA Market Cap Surpasses $4 Trillion for First Time
articles.summary: Driven by surging data-centre demand for its Blackwell GPU architecture, NVIDIA became the second company in history to exceed a $4 trillion valuation, briefly overtaking Apple.

articles.headline: Singapore's DBS Reports Record Q1 Profit, Eyes SE Asia Expansion
articles.summary: DBS Group posted a net profit of SGD 3.1 billion in Q1 2026, up 18% year-on-year, citing strong wealth management flows and rising net interest margins across its ASEAN operations.

articles.headline: Boston Dynamics' Atlas Now Handles Unstructured Warehouse Tasks Autonomously
articles.summary: The latest Atlas firmware update enables the humanoid robot to navigate cluttered environments, pick irregular items, and collaborate with human workers without pre-programmed routes.

articles.headline: Consumer Desktop Metal 3D Printer Achieves Sub-$1,000 Price Point
articles.summary: A new metal FDM printer from Desktop Metal's consumer division brings stainless steel and aluminium printing to home workshops, using a new sintering process that requires no kiln.

articles.headline: Smart Home Hub Integrates AI Vision to Cut Standby Energy Use by 40%
articles.summary: The new Matter 2.0-compatible hub uses an on-device vision model to detect room occupancy and automatically manage lighting, HVAC and plugged devices — without any cloud dependency.

articles.headline: Short-Form Video Commerce Surpasses $300B in Global Sales for 2026
articles.summary: TikTok Shop and Instagram Checkout collectively drove over $300 billion in direct-to-consumer sales in the first four months of 2026, reshaping retail strategy for brands of all sizes.

articles.headline: The 'Solo Founder' Wave: AI Tools Make One-Person Startups Viable at Scale
articles.summary: A new cohort of $1M+ ARR businesses founded and run entirely by a single person is growing rapidly, enabled by AI coding, marketing, and CRM tools that eliminate the need for early hires.

articles.headline: B2B Brands Pivot to Creator-Led Marketing as Ad ROI Declines
articles.summary: Enterprise software companies including SAP, Salesforce, and HubSpot are shifting budget to LinkedIn and YouTube creators, citing 3–5× better cost-per-lead versus traditional display advertising.

articles.headline: Salesforce Agentforce 3.0 Lets Companies Deploy Autonomous Sales Agents in Hours
articles.summary: The latest Agentforce release includes pre-built templates for outbound SDR, renewal management, and customer success, with native CRM data grounding that eliminates hallucination risk.

articles.headline: AWS Launches 'Bedrock Autopilot' — Auto-Selects the Best Model Per Task
articles.summary: Amazon's new Bedrock feature dynamically routes prompts to the optimal foundation model based on task type, latency budget, and cost target — potentially cutting cloud AI bills by 35%.

articles.headline: SAP Integrates Joule AI Copilot Into All Core ERP Modules by Default
articles.summary: SAP's AI assistant Joule is now embedded across Finance, Supply Chain, and HR modules in S/4HANA Cloud, enabling natural-language queries, anomaly alerts, and auto-drafted journal entries.

random_fact.fact: The world's first programmable electronic computer, ENIAC, weighed 27 tonnes, consumed 150 kilowatts of power, and could perform 5,000 additions per second. A modern smartphone chip executes over 15 billion operations per second — roughly 3 million times faster, in a package the size of a thumbnail.
